event_log.headline: GAME STARTED: Kanata Rangers vs Ennismore Eagles (Game #143)
event_log.detail: We need to make a strong start against Ennismore Eagles as we begin our quest to win Pool C at the OWHA U15B Provincials.

event_log.headline: GAME STARTED: Kincardine Kinucks vs Windsor Wildcats (Game #142)
event_log.detail: We need Kincardine Kinucks to win because it would prevent Windsor Wildcats from gaining early points in our pool.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kanata Rangers 1 - 0 Ennismore Eagles (in progress)
event_log.detail: With this 1-0 lead over Ennismore, we're putting ourselves in a strong position to win Pool C, as this result would have us winning the pool in 106 of 214 scenarios.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kanata Rangers 1 - 1 Ennismore Eagles (in progress)
event_log.detail: A tie in this game would put us in a strong position to win Pool C, as we'd claim first place in 38 of 225 possible scenarios.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kincardine Kinucks 1 - 0 Windsor Wildcats (in progress)
event_log.detail: This result would put us in a strong position, as we would win the pool in 48 of 214 scenarios if the score holds.

event_log.headline: FINAL: Kanata Rangers 1 - 1 Ennismore Eagles
event_log.detail: Our tie against the #25 Ennismore Eagles was a disappointing result since we were the higher-ranked team going in. With this draw, we now win Pool C in only 38 out of 243 remaining outcome combinations, significantly down from our earlier 156 out of 729 paths to victory.

event_log.headline: Score update: Kincardine Kinucks 2 - 0 Windsor Wildcats (in progress)
event_log.detail: This result puts us in a stronger position to win Pool C, as we would win the pool in 12 of 77 scenarios if this score holds.

event_log.headline: FINAL: Kincardine Kinucks 2 - 0 Windsor Wildcats
event_log.detail: This wasn't an upset - higher-ranked Kincardine (#3) beat Windsor (#41) as expected. With this result, we now win Pool C in only 12 out of 81 remaining outcome combinations, significantly down from our previous 38 out of 243 scenarios.

scouting.description: This season we've faced several teams heading to Provincials across 5 tournaments. We swept St. Catharines Brock Jr. Badgers in two meetings (6-0, 2-0). Against the rest... let's just say we're looking for redemption. Of note: Durham West Lightning (#29) is in our quarterfinal opponent's pool (Pool F) -- they beat us 2-0 in November, but they're not the favourite to win their pool. In tournament play, anything can happen!

_narrative:
**Where We Stand**
As predicted in our last update, Kincardine took care of business with a 2-0 victory over Windsor, putting the #3-ranked Kinucks at the top of Pool C with 4 points total. We remain tied for second place with Ennismore at 1 point each, while Windsor sits at the bottom with no points.

**Our Next Game**
The situation has changed since our last update - we're back to our originally scheduled 3:45 PM matchup against Windsor. The #41 Wildcats will be looking to bounce back from their shutout loss to Kincardine, making them a motivated opponent despite sitting at the bottom of the standings.

**Around the Pool**
Running simultaneously at 3:45 PM, Ennismore takes on pool-leading Kincardine in what shapes up as a crucial battle. That result will significantly impact the pool dynamics heading into tomorrow's final round of games.

_coaches_corner.don_cherry: I'll tell ya, these Kanata Rangers girls showed real heart battling back for that tie against Ennismore - that's the kind of never-give-up spirit that's gonna make 'em dangerous against Windsor! You kids out there, THAT'S what hockey's all about - these girls are growing the game the right way with pure grit!
_coaches_corner.talking_points:
**Mindset** — That tie against Ennismore shows we can compete with anyone in this tournament when we play our game. We earned a point and now we need to carry that same energy and confidence into this next game against Windsor.

**Discipline** — We've stayed completely clean with zero penalty minutes so far, which is exactly what championship teams do. Keep playing smart, physical hockey without giving them any free opportunities on the power play.

**The Opponent** — Windsor is here at provincials for a reason and they'll be hungry to make their mark. They're going to come at us hard, so we need to be ready from the first shift and stick to our systems.

**What We Need** — We're in a tight race for that pool win and only the winner advances to quarterfinals. Every shift matters now, so let's focus on playing our complete 60-minute game and getting the result we need.

_tb_health:
**Tiebreaker Health Analysis:**

We're currently tied with Ennismore on both goal differential (0) and goals against (1), meaning any tie in points with them goes to the fifth tiebreaker rule. Against other potential ties, we're behind Kincardine's +2 GD but ahead of Windsor's -2 GD and 2 GA.

Our position is fragile - a single 2-goal loss drops us to -2 GD (matching Windsor's current position), while a 2-goal win puts us at +2 GD. Since we're deadlocked with Ennismore on the primary tiebreakers, every goal scored and conceded directly impacts our standing in yellow scenarios.
